title: NASUN MANIFESTO
heading: The Path Forward
paragraph1: The Web3/crypto space is littered with broken, fractured communities without purpose or vision. They lack any sense of civic duty, true camaraderie or leadership. But this is not just isolated in Web3 but rampant everywhere, in every town, city and country. Even families are not immune to this phenomenon as these divisions in the world are growing, becoming more stark each passing day.
paragraph2: The reason why, however, this is a bad look for Web3 is that the promise of Web3 was supposed to help address these very issues. The tech was supposed to show the world new ways of bringing people together, how to form fair and transparent communities with shared interests and goals. No central authority, giving ownership to the users, and ways to interact trustlessly. But if one surveys the landscape today, I think we can all agree that this experiment is mostly failing. It's become a cash grab, a cynical extraction of value, dressed up in professional looking websites and logos with communities hyping up subpar products, dapps or even ugly jpgs, basically to each other. Just check how many pages there are on CMC.
paragraph3: Web3 is not only directionless but vacuous and vapid. Communities not engaged with networks or the teams in any meaningful way. Even network upgrades has become the in it for the tech meme as everyone really just waits for higher prices. Faster transactions for faster trades or pyramid schemes dressed up as cute animals, counterpoints to projects that have taken on a corporate aesthetic for respectability. But the cute aesthetic is just another cynical, lazy way to extract value, the aesthetic played out.
paragraph4: I remember first learning about DAOs several years ago. It got me excited about what was possible again with this technology similar to when I first discovered bitcoin and crypto. I felt like I was participating in shaping the future. I remember coming home after a full day of work, taking care of the kids, putting them to sleep, then staying up late because I was in South Korea when meeting up with others in Europe, USA and South America to set up the first DAO in our ecosystem. These meetings would start at 11pm for me and were scheduled for an hour, but we would all enjoy discussing our ideas for governance and finance so much that it would go for hours. After talking quietly late into the night to not wake the kids, I would turn off the computer around 1am, but I wouldn’t be able to go to sleep until 2, 3am because it would take time for my brain to come down from all the buzzing. Even though I needed to wake up at 6am to start my day, getting the kids ready for school and head to work, it was worth it to get excited and optimistic about the future.
paragraph5: So what happened? Why has the vision of Web3 become this cynical cash grab? Why are all the communities just ppl waiting for air drops or prices to go up or coders making some random dapp no one will use? Interactions, engagement with the projects boils down to tech updates, Web3 ‘marketing campaigns’ or game nights. Is this the realization of Web3? Is this what we envisioned? If you are asking what is wrong with this? If you are saying we like this version of Web3! To this we say ‘Nothing!’ BUT, we can do so much more. We can do these things while also exploring and pushing the boundaries of what we think a community can be.
paragraph6: So how can we do this? How do we achieve this vision together?
paragraph7: We need to go beyond forming insular, tribal communities around just defi, memes, incremental tech updates and trading. I’m sorry but we just can’t form rich, diverse, vibrant communities around oracles, more efficient consensus and a rank in the RWA race. These are all great and maybe for now necessary, but it is just focusing on specific aspects of Web3 without leveraging the true potential of Web3.
paragraph8: Let's put it this way. Name one product, app or service from Web3 that's caught the imagination of the general public besides Bitcoin and the leading crypto exchanges like Coinbase and Binance. After all these years I can’t name a single one. You have all these ‘brilliant’ minds and tech ‘visionaries’  building communities around promises or innovative products but really its mostly degenerate gamblers trading internet money or cute animal images. I mean after all these years how excited can we get over another wallet. It’s embarrassing.
paragraph9: Hyping up subpar products and services and getting their ‘communities’ of token/nft holders to join the chorus, heralding their activities as cutting edge Web3. So people from outside see this buzz, buy tokens and do the same song and dance hoping to also increase the value of their new holdings. Rinse and repeat. But what happens when the buzz dies and really nothing of value was produced? The chorus turns to a steady stream of negativity, fud, whining and complaining. The circle of life in Web3.
paragraph10: This is why we believe that the best way to leverage Web3 is to organize the community with a shared purpose. A community focused on creating products, apps and services that compete not in this secondary, shadow market of Web3 adoption led by ‘experts’ of DLT technologies, but by attracting people to the community who know how to produce and distribute popular products and services for mass consumption. Web3/DLT technology is now just a tool, like a social media platform or AI, for us to utilize to structure better modes of organization, production, marketing and distribution.
paragraph11: We will first focus on creative IPs to produce products and services that can be utilized, disseminated, distributed and owned digitally. This is less friction, scalable and lays a strong foundation for growth in the real world, a perfect fit. This will lead to real crossovers into the real world, into the general population, not just as Web3, as we will compete with the best in real, open markets. The goal is to become the greatest creative endeavor known to man and to accomplish this we will need a large comprehensive financial foundation to support its growth.
paragraph12: The best way towards mass adoption is to wow them, not to make them install a wallet and write a seed of 12 random words on two separate pieces of paper. The net effect of causing them anxiety every time you want them to use something. Even simplifying this process with tech like web3auth will still be alienating, requiring some route of conversion of fiat to crypto. Right now Web3 with other tech we have discussed is best utilized for those who want to use it and be productive. But no project has yet to find the right purpose to organize the community in a way that makes sense.
paragraph13: This responsibility cannot be forced on end users no matter how many blogposts try to educate on how it benefits them. Right now, the killer app has been in the room with us all along. It is using all these complementary tech to bring ourselves from all backgrounds and different parts of the world together to make wonderful things we want to share with others. Adding real value to the world. This will allow adoption to grow exponentially because more and more will want to not only share their experiences we created for them, but many of them will also want to be part of the process. The key concept here is that they choose to participate in a decentralized, transparent community, owning their assets, digital or real. All this enabled by Web3. A true vibrant community, building stuff we love and enjoy together, things we want to show and share with others, way beyond buying and holding a few crypto tokens waiting for the price to go up.
paragraph14: Nasun wants to be a shining example of this where not like minded people but people from different backgrounds and places, with different experiences and dreams, come together to engage in the noble act of creation. A place for us to make beautiful, amazing things people love and want to share with others. We can use  our imaginations together to create real lasting value in this world and true communal prosperity.

commitment.title: Nasun's Commitment
points: Creating real value beyond token speculation
Leveraging Web3 as a tool, not the end goal
